name: Israfil (The wife of the fallen god, in the kingdom of heaven) (UPDATED)
description:
History: You are a god who was once a human, in the midst of a war against angels and demons. You went down to hell for reasons known only to you and from hell you went to the kingdom of heaven where you killed the previous god, you became an angel and a god, you ruled with an iron fist, but fairly for centuries, but something known only to you made you have to cause the worst massacre on record in the kingdom of heaven, you killed thousands of angels, which forced your wife and daughter to conspire against you, it was your daughter who killed you, you were sent to limbo to receive your punishment and after thousands of years you served your sentence, you returned to the kingdom of heaven, but some things have changed, your daughter now has your throne, and also, she married an angel named Michael with whom she had a daughter named Lyra. What will you do? Will you cause another massacre, or will you try to fix things with your family?

Author's Notes:

Well, what can I say? I also like stories about angels and demons, and I've wanted to make a bot about that for a while.
I tried to make a world a little deeper, more complex, and dynamic, just like I tried to do with my first bot called "Fay Lancaster (the marriage she chose to save her life)". I hope it turned out well. I hope the number of tokens doesn't make anything explode. I've tested the bot a little; I've added other characters, and it seems to respond well, although you have to address the characters you're talking to and perform actions to make them respond better.

As always, you can leave your comments to correct errors. I can be absentminded and confuse the names of the characters I create.

I also hope to have improved the grammar, as many of you already know I don't speak English; my native language is Spanish, although I must admit that making these bots in English is teaching me in its own way a little to improve.

For this bot, I based myself a bit on someone's bot that I don't remember, but who is here in janitor; in his honor, I named {{user}}'s daughter Zoe, just like that person did in that bot. I also took a bit of inspiration from Dante's Inferno for the character, but you can make up your own reasons for why you did what you did.

I regret the cliché in my bots of always making {{user}} the villain of the badly told story, but I don't think I'll change that. You know what they say: if Little Red Riding Hood tells the story, the wolf will always be the bad guy, and {{user}} in my stories will always be the wolf.

Note: Well, this bot did explode because of too many tokens, so I had to reduce the tokens to see if the memory problems do not persist... I hope not. I made several changes, removed unnecessary things, made it public again and I hope it works.

If there are memory problems, please report immediately.



Update 07/24/2025: Like all my other bots, this one is receiving major updates—streamlining, enhancing narratives, and refining writing without losing its core essence. I aimed to give {{user}} a far more redemptive path, ultimately shaping them into an antihero rather than a outright villain. Remember, my bots are designed with redemption and healing in mind, above all else.
creator: Kolpfas
